input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's the full setup:

**AppShell.xaml**
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyApp.Views"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
       Title="MyApp">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page, no tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**
```csharp
namespace MyApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register routes for pages not in the visual hierarchy
        Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating to the detail page** (e.g., from `ActiveProductsPage`):
```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails", new Dictionary<string, object>
{
    ["Product"] = selectedProduct
});
```

**Receiving the parameter** in `ProductDetailsPage`:
```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(Product), "Product")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public Product Product { get; set; }
}
```

**Key points:**
- `FlyoutItem` → entry in the flyout menu.
- `Tab` inside a `FlyoutItem` → bottom tabs within that section.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` → needed for pages **not** declared in the Shell visual tree (like detail pages you push onto the nav stack).
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` → lazy-loads pages only when first visited.
